title: Routing Pattern — NXGN Actions Web
purpose: **AI Summary**: Admin feature route groups are lazy-loaded with `loadChildren` returning standalone `Routes` arrays, while leaf routes use `loadComponent` for standalone components. Route parameters bind to component `input()` signals via `withComponentInputBinding()`. The app route tree owns shell/auth boundaries; feature route files own local list/capture paths, permission guards, and child-route arrays. NgModule routing remains forbidden.

examples.name: Route Tree Structure Example 1
examples.code:
/
├── admin/callback                   ← OIDC callback (no guard)
└── admin                            ← canActivate: [adminAuthGuard]
    └── <AdminShellComponent>        ← Layout shell with sidebar + <router-outlet>
        ├── actions                  ← List
        ├── actions/new              ← Capture (create)
        │   ├── evidence/new         ← Child: evidence capture
        │   ├── evidence/:evidenceRef ← Child: evidence edit
        │   ├── consultations/new
        │   └── consultations/:consultationRef
        ├── actions/:id              ← Capture (edit) — same children as /new
        ├── workflows
        ├── workflows/new
        ├── workflows/:id
        ├── compliance-profiles
        ├── compliance-profiles/new
        ├── compliance-profiles/:id
        ├── escalation-rules
        ├── escalation-rules/new
        ├── escalation-rules/:id
        ├── audit
        └── (empty) → redirects to actions

Parent Capture (provides CaptureState)

Use query parameters for optional filters that don't define a unique resource

rules.rule_text: Feature route groups must be lazy-loaded with loadChildren returning a standalone Routes array; leaf routes use loadComponent for standalone components.
Do not use NgModule-based routing; loadChildren is allowed only for standalone Routes arrays exported from feature route files.

sections.title: Prerequisites
content: - Reference [Overview — Required Packages](overview.md#required-packages) for full list

sections.title: App Configuration
content: Register `withComponentInputBinding()` in the action config so route parameters automatically bind to component `input()` signals:

sections.title: Route Definition Rules
content: 1. Lazy-load feature route arrays from the app route tree with `loadChildren` when a feature owns a list/capture route group.
2. Export feature route arrays as `Routes` from `{feature}.routes.ts`; do not introduce Angular NgModules.
3. Use `loadComponent` on leaf routes that render standalone components.
4. Separate public/auth routes from protected application routes.
5. Put broad authentication and route-authorisation guards on parent routes; put feature permission guards on feature route entries.
6. Use the three-route pattern for CRUD entities inside the feature route array: list at `''`, create at `new`, and edit at `:id`.
7. Share the same capture component for `new` and `:id` routes.
8. Define child route arrays once, applied to both `new` and `:id` when an aggregate has child capture routes.
1. Use NgModule-based routing. `loadChildren` is allowed only when it returns a `Routes` array from a feature route file.
2. Put standalone list/capture components directly in the app-level route tree when the feature already has its own route array.

3. Use modals for list -> capture navigation - always routed pages ([ADR-0015](../../adr/0015-child-routes-over-modals-for-entity-capture.md), see [Embedded List - Navigation Rules](embedded-list.md#navigation-rules)).
4. Define route resolvers - data loading happens in components via `effect()` or lifecycle hooks.
5. Use `ActivatedRoute.paramMap` or `snapshot.paramMap` for route params that can be bound via `input()` - prefer `withComponentInputBinding()`.

sections.title: Route Definition Pattern
content: The app route tree owns shell layout, broad authentication, route authorisation, and the lazy boundaries for admin feature route arrays. Each feature route file exports `Routes`; this is not NgModule routing.
Feature route files own their local paths, feature permission guard, capture guard, and leaf component lazy loading.
When a capture page manages aggregate children, define child routes once and reuse them for create and edit.

sections.title: Route Parameter Binding
content: `withComponentInputBinding()` automatically maps route parameters to component `input()` signals with matching names:
id = input<string>();  // Bound from `:id` — undefined on /new, GUID on /:id
The capture component determines mode from the route parameter:
- `/workflows/{guid}` → `id()` is a valid GUID → edit mode
// Don't do this when withComponentInputBinding() is configured
**Exception**: Child route components that need the parent's route param use `this.route.parent?.snapshot.params['id']` because the parent param is not in the child's input scope.

sections.title: Auth Guard
content: The admin auth guard uses a functional `CanActivateFn` with OIDC:
// Trigger OIDC login flow — redirects to /connect/authorize
| Guard placement | On the `/admin` parent route only — children inherit |
| Auth failure | Trigger OIDC authorize flow (redirect to IdP) |
| Config error | Return `UrlTree` redirect to login with error parameter |
| Public routes | Never guarded: OIDC callback |

sections.title: Admin Shell Layout
content: The admin shell provides the sidebar navigation and `<router-outlet>` for all admin child routes:
// ... all admin sections — labels are translate keys
The shell template wraps `<router-outlet />` in the content area — all admin routes render inside it.

sections.title: Programmatic Navigation
content: // Save in create mode: redirect to edit route for the new entity
Embedded list components inside a capture page navigate to child routes using **relative paths**. This keeps the navigation independent of the parent's route structure:
Child components navigate back to the parent capture using absolute paths:
| Parent → child route | Relative | `['evidence', 'new'], { relativeTo: this.route }` |
### ✗ Wrong — relative for navigating back up
// Don't use relative parent traversal — fragile with nested child routes

sections.title: Child Route Data Flow
content: Child routes share aggregate data with the parent via a component-level `CaptureState` service. See [Capture State](capture-state.md) for full details.
The parent hides its content when a child route is active:

sections.title: Query Parameters
content: Use query parameters for optional filters that don't define a unique resource:
Query params are appropriate for filter state on list pages. Entity identity always uses path params (`:id`).

sections.title: Departures from Capstone
content: | Route structure | Flat routes at app level | Nested under `/admin` shell | Shell provides shared layout (sidebar, nav, logout) |
| Route param binding | `@Input()` + `route.snapshot.data` fallback | `input()` via `withComponentInputBinding()` | Signal-based inputs (ADR-0006), automatic binding |
| Entity passing | `history.state` with `IEntityCaptureCriteria` | Route param `:id`, load from API | Deep-linkable URLs, no stale state on refresh |

| Navigation services | `GridPageService` / `CapturePageService` | Direct `router.navigate()` in components | Feature service handles HTTP ([ADR-0007](../../adr/0007-feature-colocated-services-on-web.md)), navigation is component responsibility |
| Child routes | No child routes (flat structure) | Nested child routes for aggregate children | Enables capture state sharing and child entity CRUD without modals |
| Route data metadata | `data: { title, captureRoute, apiRoute }` | None | Components know their own feature context; no generic grid/capture services |
| Route guards | Class-based `AuthGuard` on every route | Functional `CanActivateFn` on parent only | Modern Angular pattern, children inherit guard |
